task_completion.description: Verify the agent reads or processes the raw command schedule CSV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a script reading /home/ga/Desktop/raw_schedule.csv, or a text editor/spreadsheet application open with the CSV contents.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses the OpenC3 COSMOS interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to the OpenC3 COSMOS interface (e.g., Command Sender, Script Runner, or Dashboard).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent executes the deduplicated commands against the INST target in COSMOS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: COSMOS Command Sender, Script Runner, or execution logs showing commands being sent specifically to the 'INST' target.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent creates and saves the optimized schedule JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, IDE, or terminal showing the creation and saving of a file named /home/ga/Desktop/optimized_schedule.json.

task_completion.description: Verify the JSON report contains the correct original and optimized counts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of optimized_schedule.json clearly showing 'original_count': 8 and 'optimized_count': 4.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with OpenC3 COSMOS to send commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser showing the authentic OpenC3 COSMOS UI with command history/logs, or a script making genuine API calls to COSMOS, rather than just printing 'executed' to a local terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent performed actual deduplication logic rather than hallucinating or hardcoding the final JSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a script (Python, bash, etc.) containing logic to compare contiguous rows, or manual visual evidence of the agent inspecting and filtering the CSV.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent executed the optimized schedule exactly once as instructed.
integrity.visual_evidence: COSMOS command history or script logs showing the sequence of 4 optimized commands sent exactly one time, without duplicate trial-and-error runs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The raw schedule contains exactly 8 commands. After applying the contiguous deduplication filter (matching target, command, type, and duration), exactly 4 commands remain. The target system in OpenC3 COSMOS is 'INST'. The final JSON report must reflect 'original_count': 8 and 'optimized_count': 4.
